behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md names binding.ts, pointsEqual, pointFrom<LocalPoint>(0, 0), @excalidraw/math, and the exact comment string asserted by tests.

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md essentially dictates the implementation: 'detect when the arrow is still in its initial state (i.e., its last point equals the origin) and return early', names the exact utility (pointsEqual), the exact constructor (pointFrom<LocalPoint>(0, 0)), and the exact comment to include.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on the specific expression 'arrow.points[arrow.points.length - 1]' and 'pointFrom<LocalPoint>(0, 0)' via regex, rejecting equivalent fixes that e.g. use a destructured last point, .at(-1), or a different zero-point construction.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Besides text checks, tests execute yarn test:typecheck, test:code, test:other, and several Vitest suites (flip, regressionTests, dragCreate, clipboard).

test_not_tautological.reason: f2p tests require the actual fix code including import and condition; a stub wouldn't include the required literal strings and pattern.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover the required comment, the pointsEqual check, import, and run drag-related test suites.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests have descriptive names and docstrings explaining fail-to-pass vs pass-to-pass intent.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is a code fix with no structured data output.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md names the exact file (packages/element/src/binding.ts), the exact utility function (pointsEqual), the exact comment to include, and describes the precise condition — removing localization and implementation reasoning from the task.
